claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's start with the first question about your child.

**How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, we can go through each one. Just let me know!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm actually asking about my three kids - Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thanks for sharing that! It's great to have all the details upfront. Since Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10) are all under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me navigate to the next step for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about their working status. 

**Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12), I'd expect they're not working, but I just need to confirm this to proceed.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are working - they're just kids at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Since Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all in school and not working, they meet this requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are all three children NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means none of them are personally claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit. Given their ages and that they're in school, this is typically the case, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, none of them get any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about apprenticeships:

**Are any of the children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently in an apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12), I would expect they're not, but I just need to confirm. If any were in an apprenticeship, certain types would affect eligibility depending on where in the UK you live.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in any apprenticeships - they're too young for that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that makes sense! Since none of them are in apprenticeships, let me move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. You can qualify through **any one** of these paths:

**Do any of the following apply to you?**

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** AND are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** AND are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** AND have sufficient resources to support yourself and family
6. You have **pre-settled status** AND are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** AND are a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

The most common situation is simply that you normally live in the UK. Does that apply to you, or does another option fit your circumstances?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, let me check if any of the other paths might apply to you.

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?** 

This would include roles such as:
- A member of the UK armed forces stationed overseas
- A civil servant posted abroad
- A diplomat or other government official working in another country

Or are you living in certain qualifying countries connected to Crown service?

If none of these apply, unfortunately the residency requirement may not be met. Could you tell me a bit more about your situation abroad?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that - I'm just living abroad with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, it appears that the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit are not met.

To be eligible for Child Benefit, the claimant must meet at least one of the residency paths, which typically require you to:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), OR
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're living abroad with your family and are not a Crown servant, you don't currently meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news. Let me now provide you with the final outcome for all three children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Outcome

**Result: NOT ELIGIBLE**

---

### Summary for Your Children:

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 7 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 12 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Charlie** | 10 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

---

### Explanation

Your children all meet the qualifying criteria for Child Benefit (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeships). However, **Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK** or meet special circumstances such as:
- Having settled or pre-settled status with qualifying conditions
- Being a Crown servant stationed abroad

Since you're currently living abroad with your family and don't fall into one of these exempt categories, you're not eligible to claim at this time.

---

### What You Can Do

- **If you return to the UK**: You may become eligible once you're normally resident in the UK again. You can make a claim at that point.
- **If your circumstances change**: For example, if you take up a Crown servant role or gain settled status, you may wish to reassess your eligibility.
- **For more information**: Visit [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility) for official guidance.

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex (age 7) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant or in another qualifying category.

Blake (age 12) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant or in another qualifying category.

Charlie (age 10) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant or in another qualifying category.
arguments.overall_reasoning: All three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) meet the qualifying child criteria for Child Benefit. However, the claimant is currently living abroad and does not fall into any of the special categories that would allow them to claim while overseas (such as being a Crown servant). UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific exemption criteria, which are not met in this case.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

Charlie (age 10) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant or in another qualifying category.
